data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 2/12/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are down by over 1% on Thursday as investors digest new labor market data amid continued weakness in tech stocks.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For the week ended February 7, initial jobless claims fell by 5,000 to 227,000, above the consensus estimate of 223,000. In addition, continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, increased by 21,000 to 1.862 million and were also above expectations of 1.85 million. The data exasperated labor market concerns, with the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announcing yesterday that the U.S. added a total of 181,000 nonfarm payrolls in 2025, down significantly from its previous estimate of 584,000.

Tech Stock Woes Continue

Tech stocks are also taking a hit, with Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), Tesla (TSLA), and Palantir (PLTR) all down by at least 2%. Within tech, software is noticeably weak on fears that AI will intensify competition and lower margins in the industry.

Despite labor market weakness, the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the March 18 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) have dropped in recent days, falling to 9.8% from 23.2% a week ago on the CME FedWatch tool. The release of January's Consumer Price Index (CPI), a key gauge of inflation, on Friday morning could send those odds surging in either direction.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Cathie Wood Buys the Post-Earnings Dip in These Stocks. Should You Follow?
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made strategic portfolio moves on Wednesday, February 11, as revealed in daily fund disclosures. The ace hedge fund manager capitalized on the post-earnings dip in Robinhood Markets (HOOD) and Shopify (SHOP) shares, accumulating millions at a discount.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wood also acquired shares of cryptocurrency companies Bullish (BLSH) and Circle Internet (CRCL) as these stocks remain pressured amid the ongoing crypto slump. Additionally, she bought shares of software company Figma (FIG), leveraging the current dip in software stocks.

Most of these purchases were made across the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW), and the ARK Fintech Innovation Fund (ARKF).

Here's How Wood Allocated $74M in Stocks

Robinhood Markets – Wood acquired a total of 433,806 shares of this brokerage platform for $33.86 million. HOOD stock plunged 8.8% on February 11 after missing Q4 revenue estimates, though earnings of $0.66 beat the consensus.

Shopify – Wood added 150,421 shares of the Canadian e-commerce portal for $17.89 million. SHOP stock dropped 6.5% yesterday, despite beating sales and earnings expectations for Q4 and announcing a $2 billion share buyback plan.

Other Notable Purchases – ARK Invest bought 364,134 shares of crypto exchange Bullish for $11.60 million. Wood added 75,559 shares of the stablecoin company Circle Internet for $4.37 million, riding a 3.2% dip in its stock price. Wood also acquired 280,138 shares of Figma for $6.32 million, while its shares declined 6.4%.

Why Wood Is Optimistic on these Stocks

With crypto markets still recovering from 2025's regulatory headwinds and software stocks facing AI-driven margin pressures, these buys signal confidence in long-term growth narratives despite short-term earnings misses, such as HOOD's revenue shortfall offset by profitability beats.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool:

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating only on Robinhood Markets, with its average price target implying an impressive 74% upside over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Tesla's Model Y Was Once Again California's Best-Selling New Vehicle in 2025
data.description:
The Tesla (TSLA) Model Y was once again California's best-selling new vehicle in 2025, its fourth straight year in the top spot. This is especially notable given the challenges that the EV maker faced during the year, including protests against CEO Elon Musk and a temporary pause in production as the company transitioned to an updated version of the Model Y. In fact, data from the California New Car Dealers Association showed that the Model Y outsold its closest competitor by more than 50,000 units.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In total, the Model Y recorded 110,120 registrations, far ahead of the Toyota (TM) RAV4 at 65,604 and the Toyota Camry at 62,324. The Tesla Model 3 also performed well by ranking fourth with 53,989 registrations, just ahead of the Honda (HMC) Civic at 53,085. That said, while the Model Y continues to lead the state, its sales have been declining each year. Registrations dropped from 132,636 in 2023 to 128,923 in 2024, and then fell again to 110,120 in 2025. At the same time, Tesla's overall sales in California have declined, falling from 238,589 in 2023 to 179,656 in 2025.

One important factor behind this slowdown is the end of the federal $7,500 EV tax credit, which had supported electric vehicle demand for years. Without that financial incentive, some buyers may have decided to delay or reconsider their purchases. Even so, Tesla still holds a strong position in the state. CNCDA President Brian Maas explained that the Tesla brand has status, especially in parts of the Bay Area, though he noted that this perception may be weakening amid political controversies surrounding Musk. Nevertheless, the continued strength of the Model Y and Model 3 highlights their overall quality.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 4.4% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Home Sales Post Largest Monthly Drop in Nearly 4 Years
data.description:
U.S. existing home sales totaled 3.91 million on a seasonally adjusted annual rate in January, falling by 8.4% compared to December and registering the largest monthly decline since February 2022. A severe winter storm that impacted more than two-thirds of the U.S. may have contributed to prospective homebuyers pushing back contract closings.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The below-normal temperatures and above-normal precipitation this January make it harder than usual to assess the underlying driver of the decrease and determine if this month's numbers are an aberration," said National Association of Realtors (NAR) Chief Economist Dr. Lawrence Yun.

Housing Inventory Shortage Keeps Prices Elevated

The slowdown comes despite the NAR's Housing Affordability Index reaching its highest level since March 2022. In addition, wage gains have outpaced home price growth, while mortgage rates have steadily declined since May. The 30-year fixed-rate mortgage currently sits at 6.09%, according to Freddie Mac.

At the same time, the median sales price of homes has remained stubbornly high, while housing inventory is still tight. The median price for a home was $396,800 in January, the highest price for the month on record.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Walmart Waves the Flag for U.S. Chocolate as it Swoons Over Valentine's Day Demand
data.description:
U.S. retailer Walmart (WMT) is hoping that waving the flag for American chocolate will leave it swooning over bumper Valentine's Day sales this weekend.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Chocolate is Top Valentine's Gift

According to Walmart, candy, especially chocolate, is the most popular Valentine's gift, chosen by more shoppers than flowers, cards, or jewelry. Indeed, more than nine in ten Americans have said that they would be more than happy to receive chocolate on Valentine's Day.

"Valentine's Day consistently ranks alongside Halloween and Easter as one of the largest chocolate-selling holidays of the year," Walmart said. "In the U.S. alone, consumers are expected to purchase roughly 58 million pounds of chocolate in the week leading up to February 14, driving billions of dollars in seasonal sales."

Because of that late rush – presumably men struggling to decide what to buy their better halves – Walmart is this year promoting the importance of U.S. chocolate manufacturing. Not just good news for those producers but also a certain sweet-toothed American who has made quite the thing about 'America First."

U.S. Commitment

Back in 2021, Walmart said it was ramping up its commitment to U.S. manufacturing with a $350 billion spend over the next 10 years.

According to Walmart, more than 2/3rds of its U.S. annual product spend is now on items are made, grown, or assembled in the United States. "That investment shows up in real ways during peak moments like Valentine's Day, when timing, availability and scale matter most.," it said. "Increasingly, this proximity matters to shoppers as well. As interest grows in where products are made and how quickly retailers can meet demand, U.S.-made chocolate is becoming part of a broader consumer shift toward accessibility."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Trump Family's WLFI Storms into the $9 Trillion Currency Market with World Swap
data.description:
World Liberty Financial (WLFI), the crypto project backed by the family of U.S. President Donald Trump, is expanding into the world of foreign exchange (FX) and remittances. The new platform, called World Swap, was announced today. The goal is to capture a piece of the $9.6 trillion that moves between different currencies every single day. The launch of World Swap on the blockchain offers a faster and much cheaper way for people to send money across borders compared to traditional banks.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

World Swap Targets High Bank Fees

The team behind the project believes that big banks and traditional money transfer services are overcharging customers. Zak Folkman, a co-founder of World Liberty Financial, explained that current players heavily tax the trillions of dollars moving globally. World Swap plans to connect users directly to their bank accounts and debit cards, allowing them to swap currencies at a fraction of the usual cost. This move follows the success of the company's lending platform, which has already seen over $300 million in activity in just its first month.

WLFI Grows despite Political Heat

This new push into currency exchange is part of a much larger plan to turn WLFI into a financial network. In January, the company applied for a national trust bank charter to gain more federal legitimacy. However, the rapid growth has brought a lot of attention from lawmakers in Washington. Democratic Representative Ro Khanna recently launched a probe into a $500 million investment from a UAE-linked group that bought nearly half of the company just before the presidential inauguration. The White House continues to deny any conflict of interest, but critics are still calling for more transparency.

Stablecoins Drive the New System

The project relies on a digital dollar called USD1, which acts as its foundation. This stablecoin has grown incredibly fast, hitting a market value of over $5 billion and becoming one of the top digital assets out there. The Trump family is already making big moves by partnering with groups in the UAE and across Asia to use this tech for international payments.

Investors can track the prices of their favorite cryptos on the TipRanks Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Slumps on Labor Market, Tech Weakness
data.description:
After opening in positive territory, the Dow Jones (DJIA) is now down by over 1% on Thursday amid labor market and tech concerns.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Department of Labor announced that initial jobless claims for the week ended February 7 fell by 5,000 to 227,000, above the consensus estimate of 223,000. The reading signaled a partial recovery from the previous week's data as the effects of a major winter storm across the U.S. eased. Furthermore, continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, increased by 21,000 to 1.862 million and were also above the expectation of 1.85 million. Initial jobless claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing jobless claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits and reflect ongoing unemployment.

The claims data build on top of January's jobs report, which seemed encouraging at first glance. Nonfarm payrolls for the month totaled 130,000, topping expectations of 65,000, while the unemployment rate fell to a six-month low of 4.3%.

However, the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) revised total nonfarm payroll gains in 2025 to 181,000 from 584,000. That averages out to 15,000 payroll additions per month, marking the lowest level outside of a recession since 2003. In addition, nonfarm payrolls continue to be driven by the healthcare and social assistance sectors, while other sectors, including government and finance, remain in decline.

Elsewhere, the U.S. rolled back tech restrictions on several Chinese firms, including router sales from TP-Link, the U.S. operations of China Unicom and China Mobile, and Chinese electric truck and bus sales, according to Reuters. The softer tech curbs may serve as a move to calm trade tensions after the U.S. and China reached a trade truce last October.

The Dow Jones is down by 1.08% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Starbucks Stock (NASDAQ:SBUX) Slips as South Korean Cafe Culture Demonstrates a Key Issue
data.description:
The push to restore coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) to its former glory as a "third place" for people to spend large amounts of time that is not at home or at work has its share of issues coming along with it. South Korean cafe culture recently demonstrated one of those points itself after an issue of seemingly abandoned property kicked in. Shareholders are watching and waiting, and Starbucks shares slipped fractionally in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reports recently noted that, in Seoul, South Korea, a group of flight attendants walked into a Starbucks, set down their bags at a set of tables, bought drinks, and then left the store. They did not take their bags with them, despite the fact that the group was reportedly large enough to occupy most of the seats in the coffee shop. Several hours later, they returned, found their bags unmolested, and left without incident.

This was not a unique incident, either. The report noted that this was the "fourth or fifth time…" that such an event had taken place in the course of several days. Customers actually walked away from the Starbucks, believing there was no room. Video of the incident went viral, and one YouTube report racked up over a million views. It also raises a point that Starbucks will need to address as it seeks to make itself a third place once more.

A Lawsuit History

Recently, Starbucks had a bit of a win in court in Missouri, as the state filed a suit against Starbucks connected to Starbucks' diversity programs. The suit alleged racism of the least-expected kind, the kind geared against white males. But the case fell apart when a judge examined it more closely and found that there had been no harm to any Missouri resident that could be found.

Chief U.S. Circuit Judge Albert Diaz wrote a concurring opinion, and noted, "Defendants represented at oral argument that there is 'absolutely' DEI activity that falls comfortably within the confines of the law." This basically took Starbucks off the firing line, at least for now.

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 15 Buys, eight Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 12.28% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $100.59 per share implies 2.24% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Will Silver Surge to $200 in 2026?
data.description:
Silver (XAGUSD) has already rallied 15% this year, outperforming both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) by a wide margin. The precious metal has benefited from a trifecta of geopolitical uncertainty, a weakening dollar, and persistent inflation. That's left some investors wondering if silver will be able to reach $200 by the end of 2026.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Most analysts believe $200 is out of reach, although they don't completely rule out the possibility. "Achieving $200 silver by 2026 is certainly possible but under extraordinary conditions and not necessarily based on current trends," said Birch Gold Group financial market strategist Peter Reagan.

Silver Rally Faces Dollar and Substitution Risks

Reagan added that the ingredients necessary for a strong silver rally include strong industrial demand, a supply-demand imbalance, and rising investment demand. Threats to the precious metal include producers turning to silver alternatives and a stronger dollar.

First Information CEO Vince Stanzione sees a path to $200 before the next presidential election in November 2028, although he warned that the scenario would likely coincide with a significant devaluation of currencies.
